EstГЎs mezclando dos cosas
Por supuesto que hay un costo financiero en esa operaciГіn pero cuando te dicen sin interГ©s es porque no lo estГЎ pagando el consumidor como un diferencial en esa cuota extra
El ejemplo claro es cuando comprГЎs un producto en MercadoLibre y sale 100 en 1 cuota pero si tenes la tarjeta de ellos lo pagГЎs 100 en 3 cuotas
Estas pagando mГЎs por comprarlo en 3 cuotas que el que lo compra en 1? No! Hay un costo financiero incluГӯdo en el precio? SI. Pero eso no te hace pagarlo mГЎs que el que lo paga en 1 cuota y ese costo financiero lo estГЎ absorbiendo MercadoLibre en ese caso comparado al que paga en 1 que le queda mГЎs plata...
O sea que lo pagГЎs menos en 3 cuotas que el que lo paga en 1...

$SE's Shopee struggled in spanish-speaking Latin America despite growing rapidly in Brazil.

Shopee launched in Brazil in late 2019. It then expanded into Mexico, Chile, and Colombia in 2021. The timing in Brazil was perfect. The pandemic pushed millions of Brazilians to shop online. Shopee scaled so fast that by 2023, and over 90% of its Brazilian sales came from local merchants rather than overseas imports. Brazil became a massive success story with a clear path to profitability.

However, the global economy quickly turned against them. Sea Limited's valuation crashed by roughly 90% due to rising interest rates and inflation. Investors stopped funding growth at all costs. They demanded real profits. Shopee had to stop burning cash immediately.

This financial pressure forced Shopee to look hard at its Spanish-speaking markets. These markets were young and bleeding money. They also featured brutal competition. $MELI had an incredible head start in the region. MercadoLibre owned a massive proprietary logistics network and the dominant digital wallet, Mercado Pago. Shopee realized it could not afford to build local warehouses and subsidize shipping just to fight MercadoLibre across multiple fragmented countries.

In late 2022, Shopee pulled the plug. Shopee fired its local teams in Mexico, Colombia, and Chile. It shifted these countries to an asset-light, cross-border model where goods ship directly from Asia. Shopee also exited Argentina completely at that time. Interestingly, Shopee testing the waters in Argentina again, but only through cross-border fulfillment. Brazil remains their only true local operation in the region. It has been a huge success story for them.

Not trying LARP as a macro speculator but as the Sunday Scaries set in I will share my thoughts on why I couldnвҖҷt care less about the macro in the long-run. 

We are living in an era of global stagflation and violent geopolitical shifts. The U.S. military intervened in Venezuela in early 2026. Middle Eastern energy shocks are driving up global freight costs. Traditional supply chains are fracturing under the pressure.

But $MELI is ready for this exact scenario. To bypass massive Atlantic shipping risks, they scaled their Full China" logistics program. They now take end-to-end control of inventory straight from Chinese factories. This effectively bypasses 3P freight forwarders and routes goods directly to Latin American consumers.

Meanwhile, Latin American fiat currencies remain highly vulnerable to inflation. MercadoLibre's answer is the Meli Dolar. This is a proprietary stablecoin pegged directly to the U.S. dollar. Users bypass legacy banking completely to protect their purchasing power. This also allows MercadoLibre to settle cross-border trades instantly and without friction.

Financially, the company is an absolute juggernaut. They are expanding their credit portfolio aggressively witha proprietary data flywheel. They offset these inherent lending risks by scaling a highly lucrative, high-margin retail media network.

MercadoLibre is entirely rewriting the rules of emerging market resilience. They aren't just reacting to the 2026 macro polycrisis. They are building the exact architecture needed to monetize it.

I can sleep well at night knowing that in the next decade the business will likely be much stronger than it is today.
